content.text:
<plugin_info kind="matched_installed">
display_name: Cloudflare
description: Skills for the Cloudflare developer platform: Workers, Durable Objects, Agents SDK, MCP servers, Wrangler CLI, and web performance
skills:
  - agents-sdk: Build AI agents on Cloudflare Workers using the Agents SDK. Load when creating stateful agents, durable workflows, real-time WebSocket apps, scheduled tasks, MCP servers, or chat applications. Covers Agent class, state management, callable RPC, Workflows integration, and React hooks.
  - building-ai-agent-on-cloudflare: Builds AI agents on Cloudflare using the Agents SDK with state management,
real-time WebSockets, scheduled tasks, tool integration, and chat capabilities.
Generates production-ready agent code deployed to Workers.

Use when: user wants to "build an agent", "AI agent", "chat agent", "stateful
agent", mentions "Agents SDK", needs "real-time AI", "WebSocket AI", or asks
about agent "state management", "scheduled tasks", or "tool calling".
  - building-mcp-server-on-cloudflare: Builds remote MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers on Cloudflare Workers
with tools, OAuth authentication, and production deployment. Generates
server code, configures auth providers, and deploys to Workers.

Use when: user wants to "build MCP server", "create MCP tools", "remote
MCP", "deploy MCP", add "OAuth to MCP", or mentions Model Context Protocol
on Cloudflare. Also triggers on "MCP authentication" or "MCP deployment".
  - cloudflare: Comprehensive Cloudflare platform skill covering Workers, Pages, storage (KV, D1, R2), AI (Workers AI, Vectorize, Agents SDK), networking (Tunnel, Spectrum), security (WAF, DDoS), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi). Use for any Cloudflare development task.
  - durable-objects: Create and review Cloudflare Durable Objects. Use when building stateful coordination (chat rooms, multiplayer games, booking systems), implementing RPC methods, SQLite storage, alarms, WebSockets, or reviewing DO code for best practices. Covers Workers integration, wrangler config, and testing with Vitest.
  - sandbox-sdk: Build sandboxed applications for secure code execution. Load when building AI code execution, code interpreters, CI/CD systems, interactive dev environments, or executing untrusted code. Covers Sandbox SDK lifecycle, commands, files, code interpreter, and preview URLs.
  - web-perf: Analyzes web performance using Chrome DevTools MCP. Measures Core Web Vitals (FCP, LCP, TBT, CLS, Speed Index), identifies render-blocking resources, network dependency chains, layout shifts, caching issues, and accessibility gaps. Use when asked to audit, profile, debug, or optimize page load performance, Lighthouse scores, or site speed.
  - workers-best-practices: Reviews and authors Cloudflare Workers code against production best practices. Load when writing new Workers, reviewing Worker code, configuring wrangler.jsonc, or checking for common Workers anti-patterns (streaming, floating promises, global state, secrets, bindings, observability). Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
  - wrangler: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices.
rules:
  - workers: When building applications on Cloudflare Workers or with frameworks that deploy to Cloudflare Workers, strongly prefer retrieval and fetching documentation over training data.
commands:
  - build-agent: Build an AI agent on Cloudflare using the Agents SDK
  - build-mcp: Build a remote MCP server on Cloudflare with tools and OAuth
mcp_servers:
  - cloudflare-docs
  - cloudflare-bindings
  - cloudflare-builds
  - cloudflare-observability
</plugin_info>
<user_query>
Make a plan for implementing our "Preview Deployment" workflows. These workflows
are used so that each PR gets its own neon database and cloudflare worker
deploy.

There should be two workflows:

1. `deploy-preview.yml`

- Runs on `pull_request`
- Get the github branch name
- Create a `preview/git-branch-name` neon with `staging` as the parent
- Run migrations
- Deploy `api` to cloudflare passing the database url
- Build `web` for preview with api url
- Deploy `web` to cloudflare 
- Comment on the PR with a link to the created neon DB, a link to the
`/reference` docs for the api, and a link to the frontend site

2. `cleanup-preview.yml`

- runs on pull request closed
- delete the `api` and `web` deployments and delete the neon db for the pull
request
</user_query>

Exploring the codebase for existing CI/CD, Neon, and Cloudflare deployment patterns to draft an accurate plan.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Noting a key constraint: worker-level secrets on shared `api-staging` can't isolate per-PR database URLs—planning dedicated preview workers per PR.

[REDACTED]

questions.prompt: For Cloudflare, per-PR DATABASE_URL isolation requires separate Worker scripts (shared staging Workers reuse one secret). Which approach do you want?

options.label: Dedicated Workers per PR (api-preview-<slug>, web-preview-<slug>) — wrangler delete on cleanup (recommended)
Preview aliases on existing api-staging / web-staging via wrangler versions upload --preview-alias (simpler URLs, weaker DB isolation unless we solve per-version secrets)

input.name: Preview deployment workflows
input.overview: Add two GitHub Actions workflows that provision a Neon branch per PR, deploy isolated `api-preview-*` and `web-preview-*` Cloudflare Workers, comment preview links on the PR, and tear everything down when the PR closes.
input.plan:
# Preview deployment workflows

## Architecture

Each pull request gets three isolated resources:

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph pr [Pull request]
    PR[PR opened / sync]
  end
  subgraph neon [Neon]
    Staging[staging branch]
    PreviewBranch["preview/head_ref"]
  end
  subgraph cf [Cloudflare Workers]
    ApiWorker["api-preview-slug"]
    WebWorker["web-preview-slug"]
  end
  PR --> CreateBranch
  CreateBranch --> PreviewBranch
  Staging --> PreviewBranch
  PreviewBranch --> Migrate
  Migrate --> DeployApi
  DeployApi --> ApiWorker
  DeployApi --> BuildWeb
  BuildWeb --> DeployWeb
  DeployWeb --> WebWorker
  DeployWeb --> Comment
  PR_closed[PR closed] --> DeleteWorkers
  PR_closed --> DeleteBranch
```

**Why dedicated Workers:** You chose per-PR Workers so `DATABASE_URL` and other secrets are not shared on `api-staging` / `web-staging` (Worker secrets are script-level, not per preview version).

**Naming (shared between workflows):** Add [`.github/scripts/preview-names.sh`](.github/scripts/preview-names.sh) that reads `GITHUB_HEAD_REF` and prints (via `$GITHUB_OUTPUT` or stdout):

| Output | Rule | Example (`feature/add-auth`) |
|--------|------|------------------------------|
| `neon_branch` | `preview/${GITHUB_HEAD_REF}` | `preview/feature/add-auth` |
| `cf_slug` | lowercase; non `[a-z0-9-]` → `-`; trim; max ~40 chars; must start with `[a-z]` | `feature-add-auth` |
| `api_worker` | `api-preview-${cf_slug}` | `api-preview-feature-add-auth` |
| `web_worker` | `web-preview-${cf_slug}` | `web-preview-feature-add-auth` |

Enforce Cloudflare preview-alias rules on `cf_slug` (letters, numbers, dashes; starts with letter; combined worker name ≤ 63 chars).

**Predictable URLs (for CORS + build):** Add a repo variable `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` (account workers.dev subdomain, e.g. from an existing `api-staging` URL). Then:

- API: `https://${api_worker}.${CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN}.workers.dev`
- Web: `https://${web_worker}.${CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN}.workers.dev`
- API docs: `${API_URL}/reference` ([`apps/api/README.md`](apps/api/README.md))

Update [`DECISION_RECORD.md`](DECISION_RECORD.md) Neon section: preview branches use parent **`staging`** (your spec; doc currently says parent `preview`).

---

## Prerequisites (one-time repo setup)

| Name | Type | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `NEON_API_KEY` | secret | Neon create/delete branch actions |
| `NEON_PROJECT_ID` | variable | Neon project |
| `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` | secret | Already used in [`deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml) |
| `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` | secret | Same |
| `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` | variable | Build predictable `*.workers.dev` URLs |
| `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN` | secret | API logging (reuse staging value) |
| `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` | secret (optional) | Web preview auth; omit if previews are unauthenticated |

Optional: GitHub **environment** `preview` on the deploy job for approval gates / scoped secrets.

---

## 1. [`deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml)

**Triggers:** `pull_request` (`opened`, `synchronize`, `reopened`)

**Permissions:** `contents: read`, `pull-requests: write` (PR comment)

**Concurrency:**

```yaml
group: preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
cancel-in-progress: true
```

**Job steps (mirror toolchain from [`deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml)):**

1. **Checkout** + pnpm/node setup (Node `25.3.0`, pnpm `10.20.0`, frozen lockfile).
2. **Compute names** — run `preview-names.sh` → `neon_branch`, `cf_slug`, `api_worker`, `web_worker`, `api_url`, `web_url`.
3. **Neon branch** — [`neondatabase/create-branch-action@v6`](https://github.com/neondatabase/create-branch-action):
   - `branch_name: ${{ neon_branch }}`
   - `parent_branch: staging`
   - `project_id: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}`
   - `api_key: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}`
   - Use output `db_url` (pooled) for migrations + API secret.
   - Use output `branch_id` for Neon console link in PR comment.
4. **Migrations** — from `apps/api`:

```yaml
env:
  DATABASE_URL: ${{ steps.create-branch.outputs.db_url }}
run: pnpm --filter api run db:migrate
```

([`drizzle.config.ts`](apps/api/drizzle.config.ts) already requires `DATABASE_URL` for `db:migrate`.)

5. **Deploy API** — dedicated Worker (not `--env staging`):

```bash
# secrets are script-scoped to api_worker
echo "$DATABASE_URL" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put DATABASE_URL --name "$API_WORKER"
echo "$LOGTAIL_TOKEN" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN --name "$API_WORKER"
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put CORS_ORIGIN --name "$API_WORKER" <<< "$WEB_URL"
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put ENVIRONMENT --name "$API_WORKER" <<< "preview"
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put LOG_LEVEL --name "$API_WORKER" <<< "info"
pnpm --filter api run deploy -- --name "$API_WORKER" --minify
```

6. **Build web for preview** — pass API URL at build time:

```yaml
env:
  VITE_API_URL: ${{ env.api_url }}
run: pnpm exec turbo build --filter=web
```

7. **Deploy web** — same pattern as staging deploy, with dynamic name:

```bash
pnpm --filter web exec wrangler deploy --config dist/server/wrangler.json --name "$WEB_WORKER"
```

Optionally set `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` via `wrangler vars put` on `$WEB_WORKER` if secret is configured.

8. **PR comment** — [`peter-evans/create-or-update-comment@v4`](https://github.com/peter-evans/create-or-update-comment) with a stable `comment-id` (e.g. `preview-deployment`) and body:

- Neon branch: link to `https://console.neon.tech/app/projects/${NEON_PROJECT_ID}/branches/${branch_id}`
- API reference: `${api_url}/reference`
- Frontend: `${web_url}`

Do **not** re-run Biome here; [`ci.yml`](.github/workflows/ci.yml) already gates PRs.

---

## 2. [`cleanup-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml)

**Triggers:** `pull_request` `types: [closed]`

**Steps:**

1. Checkout + compute names (same `preview-names.sh`).
2. **Delete Cloudflare Workers** (idempotent; ignore missing):

```bash
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler delete "$API_WORKER" --force || true
pnpm --filter web exec wrangler delete "$WEB_WORKER" --force || true
```

3. **Delete Neon branch** — [`neondatabase/delete-branch-action@v3`](https://github.com/neondatabase/delete-branch-action):

```yaml
with:
  project_id: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}
  branch: ${{ neon_branch }}
  api_key: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}
```

4. **Optional:** update/remove the preview PR comment (delete or strikethrough “Preview torn down”).

---

## Small code/config changes (supporting)

### API — allow `ENVIRONMENT=preview`

In [`apps/api/src/env.ts`](apps/api/src/env.ts), extend the enum:

```ts
.enum(["development", "staging", "production", "preview", "test"])
```

Regenerate types if you run `pnpm --filter api cf-typegen` after binding changes.

### Web — build-time API URL

In [`apps/web/src/env.ts`](apps/web/src/env.ts), add client env (used when the app starts calling the API):

```ts
client: {
  VITE_API_URL: z.url(),
},
```

Document in [`apps/web/README.md`](apps/web/README.md) that preview CI sets `VITE_API_URL` at build time.

(No change required to [`apps/api/wrangler.jsonc`](apps/api/wrangler.jsonc) / [`apps/web/wrangler.jsonc`](apps/web/wrangler.jsonc) staging/production envs; preview uses `--name` overrides only.)

---

## Operational notes

- **Re-runs:** `create-branch-action` reuses an existing branch if present; migrations remain safe to re-run.
- **Long branch names:** truncate `cf_slug` so `api-preview-*` stays within Worker/DNS limits.
- **First deploy:** use `wrangler deploy` (not `versions upload`) for net-new preview Workers ([Cloudflare docs](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/versions-and-deployments/)).
- **Security:** never echo `db_url` in logs; pass only to `DATABASE_URL` env / `wrangler secret put` stdin.
- **Manual fallback:** document `neonctl branches delete` and `wrangler delete` with computed names if Actions fail.

---

## Verification checklist

After implementation:

1. Open a test PR → workflow creates Neon branch, migrates, deploys both Workers, comments links.
2. Push to PR → concurrency cancels prior run; new commit redeploys same Workers/branch.
3. Close PR → Workers deleted, Neon branch deleted.
4. Hit `${api_url}/reference` and `${web_url}` from the comment; confirm API uses preview DB (e.g. isolated hello data).

todos.content: Add .github/scripts/preview-names.sh (neon branch, cf slug, worker names, URLs)
Create .github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml (Neon branch, migrate, deploy api/web, PR comment)
Create .github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml (wrangler delete workers, Neon delete branch)
Add ENVIRONMENT=preview to api env schema; add VITE_API_URL to web env.ts
Update DECISION_RECORD.md (preview parent staging) and document required GitHub secrets/vars

**AGENTS.md** (was empty):

**Learned User Preferences**
- Merge to `master` → auto staging deploy; production requires manual approval (not auto after staging)
- Each PR gets its own Cloudflare preview + Neon preview DB
- Preserve Wrangler setup when porting/scaffolding apps

**Learned Workspace Facts**
- pnpm monorepo: `workspace:*` vs `catalog:` conventions
- `apps/api` (Hono Workers) vs `apps/web` (TanStack Start, deploy via `dist/server/wrangler.json`)
- Worker naming: `api-staging` / `api-production`, `web-staging` / `web-production`
- `deploy.yml` branch/deploy mapping
- Neon branch hierarchy (`prod`, `staging`, `dev`, `preview/<branch>`)

**Index maintenance**

- Indexed all 10 parent transcripts with current `mtimeMs`
- No stale entries removed (first run; none missing on disk)

todos.content: Add .github/scripts/preview-names.sh (neon branch, cf slug, worker names, URLs)

input.contents:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Compute Neon branch name, Cloudflare Worker names, and preview URLs for a PR.
# Requires: GITHUB_HEAD_REF, CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN
# Writes to GITHUB_OUTPUT when set (GitHub Actions).
set -euo pipefail

HEAD_REF="${GITHUB_HEAD_REF:-}"
if [[ -z "$HEAD_REF" ]]; then
  echo "GITHUB_HEAD_REF is required" >&2
  exit 1
fi

SUBDOMAIN="${CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN:-}"
if [[ -z "$SUBDOMAIN" ]]; then
  echo "CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN is required" >&2
  exit 1
fi

NEON_BRANCH="preview/${HEAD_REF}"

CF_SLUG=$(echo "$HEAD_REF" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed -E 's/[^a-z0-9]+/-/g; s/^-+|-+$//g')
if [[ -z "$CF_SLUG" ]]; then
  CF_SLUG="branch"
fi
if [[ ! "$CF_SLUG" =~ ^[a-z] ]]; then
  CF_SLUG="b-${CF_SLUG}"
fi
# Keep api-preview-* / web-preview-* within Worker name limits (63 chars).
CF_SLUG="${CF_SLUG:0:40}"
CF_SLUG=$(echo "$CF_SLUG" | sed -E 's/-+$//')
if [[ -z "$CF_SLUG" ]]; then
  CF_SLUG="branch"
fi

API_WORKER="api-preview-${CF_SLUG}"
WEB_WORKER="web-preview-${CF_SLUG}"
API_URL="https://${API_WORKER}.${SUBDOMAIN}.workers.dev"
WEB_URL="https://${WEB_WORKER}.${SUBDOMAIN}.workers.dev"

write_output() {
  local key="$1"
  local value="$2"
  if [[ -n "${GITHUB_OUTPUT:-}" ]]; then
    {
      echo "${key}<<EOF"
      echo "$value"
      echo "EOF"
    } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  else
    echo "${key}=${value}"
  fi
}

if [[ -n "${GITHUB_OUTPUT:-}" ]]; then
  write_output "neon_branch" "$NEON_BRANCH"
  write_output "cf_slug" "$CF_SLUG"
  write_output "api_worker" "$API_WORKER"
  write_output "web_worker" "$WEB_WORKER"
  write_output "api_url" "$API_URL"
  write_output "web_url" "$WEB_URL"
else
  echo "neon_branch=${NEON_BRANCH}"
  echo "cf_slug=${CF_SLUG}"
  echo "api_worker=${API_WORKER}"
  echo "web_worker=${WEB_WORKER}"
  echo "api_url=${API_URL}"
  echo "web_url=${WEB_URL}"
fi

todos.content: Add .github/scripts/preview-names.sh (neon branch, cf slug, worker names, URLs)
Create .github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml (Neon branch, migrate, deploy api/web, PR comment)

input.contents:
name: Deploy preview

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

concurrency:
  group: preview-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  deploy-preview:
    name: Deploy preview
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      CI: true
      GITHUB_HEAD_REF: ${{ github.head_ref }}
      CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN: ${{ vars.CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN }}
      CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
      CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 10.20.0

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 25.3.0
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Compute preview names
        id: names
        run: ./.github/scripts/preview-names.sh

      - name: Create Neon preview branch
        id: neon
        uses: neondatabase/create-branch-action@v6
        with:
          project_id: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}
          branch_name: ${{ steps.names.outputs.neon_branch }}
          parent_branch: staging
          api_key: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}

      - name: Run database migrations
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ steps.neon.outputs.db_url }}
        run: pnpm --filter api run db:migrate

      - name: Deploy API preview Worker
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: ${{ steps.neon.outputs.db_url }}
          API_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.api_worker }}
          WEB_URL: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_url }}
          LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          echo "$DATABASE_URL" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put DATABASE_URL --name "$API_WORKER"
          echo "$LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN --name "$API_WORKER"
          echo "$WEB_URL" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put CORS_ORIGIN --name "$API_WORKER"
          echo "preview" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put ENVIRONMENT --name "$API_WORKER"
          echo "info" | pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret put LOG_LEVEL --name "$API_WORKER"
          pnpm --filter api run deploy -- --name "$API_WORKER" --minify

      - name: Build web for preview
        env:
          VITE_API_URL: ${{ steps.names.outputs.api_url }}
        run: pnpm exec turbo build --filter=web

      - name: Deploy web preview Worker
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
        run: pnpm --filter web exec wrangler deploy --config dist/server/wrangler.json --name "$WEB_WORKER"

      - name: Set WorkOS client ID on web preview
        if: secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID != ''
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
          WORKOS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID }}
        run: |
          pnpm --filter web exec wrangler vars put WORKOS_CLIENT_ID "$WORKOS_CLIENT_ID" --name "$WEB_WORKER"

      - name: Comment preview links on PR
        uses: peter-evans/create-or-update-comment@v4
        with:
          issue-number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          comment-id: preview-deployment
          body: |
            ## Preview deployment

            | Resource | Link |
            | --- | --- |
            | Neon branch | [${{ steps.names.outputs.neon_branch }}](https://console.neon.tech/app/projects/${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}/branches/${{ steps.neon.outputs.branch_id }}) |
            | API reference | [${{ steps.names.outputs.api_url }}/reference](${{ steps.names.outputs.api_url }}/reference) |
            | Frontend | [${{ steps.names.outputs.web_url }}](${{ steps.names.outputs.web_url }}) |

            Workers: `${{ steps.names.outputs.api_worker }}`, `${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}`

name: Cleanup preview

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [closed]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  cleanup-preview:
    name: Cleanup preview
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      CI: true
      GITHUB_HEAD_REF: ${{ github.head_ref }}
      CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN: ${{ vars.CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN }}
      CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN }}
      CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID: ${{ secrets.CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 10.20.0

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 25.3.0
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Compute preview names
        id: names
        run: ./.github/scripts/preview-names.sh

      - name: Delete API preview Worker
        env:
          API_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.api_worker }}
        run: pnpm --filter api exec wrangler delete "$API_WORKER" --force || true

      - name: Delete web preview Worker
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
        run: pnpm --filter web exec wrangler delete "$WEB_WORKER" --force || true

      - name: Delete Neon preview branch
        uses: neondatabase/delete-branch-action@v3
        with:
          project_id: ${{ vars.NEON_PROJECT_ID }}
          branch: ${{ steps.names.outputs.neon_branch }}
          api_key: ${{ secrets.NEON_API_KEY }}

      - name: Mark preview comment as torn down
        uses: peter-evans/create-or-update-comment@v4
        with:
          issue-number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          comment-id: preview-deployment
          body: |
            ## Preview deployment (torn down)

            Preview resources for branch `${{ github.head_ref }}` were removed when this pull request closed.

input.old_string:
- name: Set WorkOS client ID on web preview
        if: secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID != ''
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
          WORKOS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID }}
        run: |
          pnpm --filter web exec wrangler vars put WORKOS_CLIENT_ID "$WORKOS_CLIENT_ID" --name "$WEB_WORKER"
input.new_string:
- name: Set WorkOS client ID on web preview
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
          WORKOS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID }}
        run: |
          if [[ -n "${WORKOS_CLIENT_ID:-}" ]]; then
            pnpm --filter web exec wrangler vars put WORKOS_CLIENT_ID "$WORKOS_CLIENT_ID" --name "$WEB_WORKER"
          fi

input.new_string:
Set `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` (and optionally `WORKOS_API_HOSTNAME`) on each `web-*` Worker in Cloudflare, or copy `apps/web/.dev.vars.example` to `.dev.vars` for local deploy.

## Pull request preview deployments

Workflows [`.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml) and [`.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml) run on every pull request. Each PR gets:

- A Neon branch `preview/<head-ref>` (parent: `staging`)
- Dedicated Workers `api-preview-<slug>` and `web-preview-<slug>`
- A PR comment with Neon console, API `/reference`, and frontend links

Configure these GitHub **secrets** and **variables** before enabling previews:

| Name | Type | Purpose |
|------|------|---------|
| `NEON_API_KEY` | secret | Neon create/delete branch actions |
| `NEON_PROJECT_ID` | variable | Neon project ID |
| `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` | secret | Wrangler deploy (same as staging/production) |
| `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` | secret | Wrangler account |
| `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` | variable | Account `workers.dev` subdomain (from any `*.workers.dev` URL) |
| `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN` | secret | API logging on preview Workers |
| `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` | secret (optional) | Web preview auth |

`CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` is the label between the worker name and `.workers.dev` (e.g. for `https://api-staging.<subdomain>.workers.dev`, set the variable to `<subdomain>`).

For **PR preview** builds, CI sets `VITE_API_URL` to the preview API Worker URL at build time (see root [README](../../README.md#pull-request-preview-deployments)).

## Setting up WorkOS

content.text:
Preview deployment workflows are in place. Summary:

### New files
- [`.github/scripts/preview-names.sh`](.github/scripts/preview-names.sh) — derives `neon_branch`, `cf_slug`, worker names, and `*.workers.dev` URLs from `GITHUB_HEAD_REF`
- [`.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml) — Neon branch (parent `staging`), migrations, dedicated `api-preview-*` / `web-preview-*` Workers, PR comment
- [`.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml) — deletes Workers and Neon branch on PR close, updates the comment

### Code / docs
- [`apps/api/src/env.ts`](apps/api/src/env.ts) — `ENVIRONMENT` includes `preview`
- [`apps/web/src/env.ts`](apps/web/src/env.ts) — optional `VITE_API_URL` for preview builds
- [`DECISION_RECORD.md`](DECISION_RECORD.md) — preview branches parent is `staging`
- [`README.md`](README.md) — prerequisites table for GitHub secrets/variables
- [`apps/web/README.md`](apps/web/README.md) — notes `VITE_API_URL` in preview CI

### Before first PR preview
Set in GitHub: `NEON_API_KEY`, `NEON_PROJECT_ID`, `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN`, `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN`, plus existing Cloudflare secrets. Optional: `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID`.

`CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` is the account subdomain segment (e.g. `anedot` from `https://api-staging.anedot.workers.dev`).

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
Implement the plan as specified, it is attached for your reference. Do NOT edit the plan file itself.

To-do's from the plan have already been created. Do not create them again. Mark them as in_progress as you work, starting with the first one. Don't stop until you have completed all the to-dos.
</user_query>

<user_query>
where should the CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN come from?
</user_query>

Checking how the repo documents and uses this value.

[REDACTED]

`CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` is **not** generated by the workflow or Wrangler. It is a **one-time GitHub repository variable** you set manually from your Cloudflare account’s `workers.dev` hostname.

## What it is

Cloudflare serves Workers on URLs like:

```text
https://<worker-name>.<account-subdomain>.workers.dev
```

`CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` is that middle segment — the **account-level** `workers.dev` subdomain. It is the same for every Worker in the account.

Preview CI uses it in [`.github/scripts/preview-names.sh`](.github/scripts/preview-names.sh) to build predictable URLs **before** deploy (for CORS and `VITE_API_URL`), for example:

```text
https://api-preview-feature-add-auth.<subdomain>.workers.dev
```

## Where to get it

1. Open **Cloudflare dashboard** → **Workers & Pages** → pick an existing Worker (e.g. `api-staging` or `web-staging` after a staging deploy).
2. Copy its `workers.dev` URL from **Settings → Domains & Routes** or the worker overview.
3. Take the segment **between** the worker name and `.workers.dev`.

Example: if the URL is:

```text
https://api-staging.anedot.workers.dev
```

then set the GitHub variable to:

```text
anedot
```

(not `api-staging` — that is the worker name, not the account subdomain).

## Where to put it

**GitHub** → repo **Settings** → **Secrets and variables** → **Actions** → **Variables** → **New repository variable**:

- Name: `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN`
- Value: e.g. `anedot`

The preview workflows read it via `vars.CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` in [`deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml) and [`cleanup-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml).

## If you do not have a staging Worker yet

Deploy staging once (or any Worker with `workers.dev` enabled), then read the subdomain from that URL. Until you have a real `*.workers.dev` URL, the preview workflow cannot construct correct links or CORS origins.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
what other variables/secrets do I need to ensure are in github actions settings?
</user_query>

Here is what this repo’s workflows expect in **GitHub → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions**.

## Preview workflows only

These are used by [`deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml) and [`cleanup-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/cleanup-preview.yml):

| Name | Type | Required? | Used for |
|------|------|-----------|----------|
| `NEON_API_KEY` | **Secret** | Yes | Create/delete Neon branch (`preview/<branch>`, parent `staging`) |
| `NEON_PROJECT_ID` | **Variable** | Yes | Neon project ID (console → Project → Settings) |
| `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` | **Secret** | Yes | Wrangler deploy/delete preview Workers |
| `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` | **Secret** | Yes | Wrangler account ID |
| `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN` | **Variable** | Yes | Build `https://<worker>.<subdomain>.workers.dev` URLs before deploy |
| `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN` | **Secret** | Yes (for API preview) | `DATABASE_URL` sibling secrets on `api-preview-*` Workers |
| `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` | **Secret** | No | Web preview auth; workflow skips if empty |

**Cleanup** needs the same set except `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN` and `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` (it only deletes Workers and the Neon branch).

## Already used by staging/production deploy

[`deploy.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy.yml) uses:

| Name | Type |
|------|------|
| `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` | Secret |
| `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` | Secret |

If staging/production deploys already work, you likely have these. Preview reuses them.

`deploy.yml` does **not** set Worker secrets in CI — staging/production `DATABASE_URL`, `LOGTAIL_*`, `CORS_ORIGIN`, etc. are expected to be configured in Cloudflare (dashboard or manual `wrangler secret put`) per [`apps/api/README.md`](apps/api/README.md).

## CI workflow

[`ci.yml`](.github/workflows/ci.yml) needs **no** secrets or variables (checkout + `pnpm check` only).

---

## Where to get each value

- **`NEON_API_KEY`** — [Neon console](https://console.neon.tech) → Account → API keys, or Neon GitHub integration (can create this for you).
- **`NEON_PROJECT_ID`** — Neon project → Settings → Project ID.
- **`CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`** — Cloudflare dashboard → My Profile → API Tokens. Needs Workers Scripts edit/deploy (and delete for cleanup). Often scoped to your account.
- **`CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`** — Cloudflare dashboard → any zone/account overview (right sidebar) or from `wrangler whoami` when logged in.
- **`CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN`** — Middle segment of any `*.workers.dev` URL (e.g. `anedot` from `https://api-staging.anedot.workers.dev`).
- **`LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN`** — Better Stack Logs → JavaScript source → source token (same as local [`apps/api/.dev.vars.example`](apps/api/.dev.vars.example)).
- **`WORKOS_CLIENT_ID`** — WorkOS dashboard → your AuthKit/OAuth app (only if you want auth on preview web).

---

## Minimal checklist

**Secrets (repository):**

1. `NEON_API_KEY`
2. `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN`
3. `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID`
4. `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN`
5. `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` (optional)

**Variables (repository):**

1. `NEON_PROJECT_ID`
2. `CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_DEV_SUBDOMAIN`

Nothing else is referenced in `.github/workflows/` today. Optional GitHub **environment** `preview` is not wired in the workflows yet; secrets live at repo level unless you add `environment: preview` to the jobs later.

[REDACTED]

  See https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/secrets/#secrets-on-deployed-workers for more information.

🪵  Logs were written to "/home/runner/.config/.wrangler/logs/wrangler-2026-05-20_15-03-37_807.log"
/home/runner/work/anedot-one/anedot-one/apps/api:
 ERR_PNPM_RECURSIVE_RUN_FIRST_FAIL  api@0.1.0 deploy: `wrangler deploy --minify -- --name api-preview-dan-api-neon-setup --minify`
Exit status 1
Error: Process completed with exit code 1.

Investigating the deploy command and how Wrangler validates secrets for preview Workers.

[REDACTED]

Identifying the issue: `pnpm run deploy -- --name ...` produces `wrangler deploy --minify -- --name ...`, so `--name` isn't applied and deploy targets the default worker `api` instead of the preview worker where secrets were set. Verifying and fixing the workflow.

[REDACTED]

input.search_term: wrangler secret put --name worker before first deploy new worker
input.explanation: Confirm secret put --name works for not-yet-deployed workers.

content.text:
Identifying the bug: `pnpm run deploy -- --name ...` doesn't reliably pass `--name` to Wrangler (error shows `wrangler deploy --minify -- --name ...`), so deploy targets the default worker `api` while secrets were set on `api-preview-*`. Fixing by calling `wrangler deploy` directly with `--name`.

[REDACTED]

The failure is almost certainly a **worker name mismatch**, not missing GitHub secrets.

## What’s going wrong

1. CI runs `wrangler secret put … --name api-preview-dan-api-neon-setup` — secrets go on the **preview** worker.
2. CI runs `pnpm --filter api run deploy -- --name "$API_WORKER" --minify`, which becomes something like:
   ```bash
   wrangler deploy --minify -- --name api-preview-dan-api-neon-setup --minify
   ```
3. That `--` often means **`--name` is not applied**, so deploy targets the default script from [`apps/api/wrangler.jsonc`](apps/api/wrangler.jsonc): **`api`**, not `api-preview-*`.
4. Wrangler then checks `secrets.required` on **`api`**, which has no secrets in this job → the error you see.

So the secrets step can succeed while deploy still fails.

## Fix

In [`.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml), replace the deploy line with a direct `wrangler` call (same pattern as web deploy):

```yaml
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler deploy --minify --name "$API_WORKER"
```

Remove `run deploy -- --name …` so you don’t go through the `package.json` script and pnpm’s `--` forwarding.

## Optional hardening

- After the secret puts, add a quick check:
  ```bash
  pnpm --filter api exec wrangler secret list --name "$API_WORKER"
  ```
- Add a dedicated script in `apps/api/package.json`, e.g. `"deploy:worker": "wrangler deploy --minify"`, and call:
  ```bash
  pnpm --filter api run deploy:worker -- --name "$API_WORKER"
  ```
  (only if you verify pnpm forwards args without an extra `--`).

## GitHub secrets

Still required in Actions: `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN` (and the rest from the preview table). If that secret were empty, `secret put` could fail too — but your log points at deploy-time validation on the **wrong** worker name.

I’m in plan mode and can’t edit the workflow file from here. Switch to **Agent** mode and ask to apply this fix, or change that one line in `deploy-preview.yml` yourself and re-run the workflow.

[REDACTED]

Updated [`.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml`](.github/workflows/deploy-preview.yml): the API deploy step now runs:

```bash
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler deploy --minify --name "$API_WORKER"
```

so deploy targets the same preview worker where secrets were set. Push or re-run the workflow on your PR to verify.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
explain how this fixes the issue with so many env vars missing?
</user_query>

The “missing secrets” message was misleading. The secrets were probably set correctly; deploy was just checking the **wrong Worker**.

## Two different Workers

| Step | Command (before) | Which Worker |
|------|------------------|--------------|
| `secret put` | `wrangler secret put … --name api-preview-…` | `api-preview-dan-api-neon-setup` |
| `deploy` | `pnpm run deploy` → `wrangler deploy --minify` (no effective `--name`) | **`api`** (default in `wrangler.jsonc`) |

[`apps/api/wrangler.jsonc`](apps/api/wrangler.jsonc) sets `"name": "api"`. Preview CI never overrode that on deploy, so Wrangler deployed and validated **`api`**, not the preview worker.

Secrets live **per Worker script**. Putting them on `api-preview-…` does nothing for `api`. Hence:

```text
The following required secrets have not been set: DATABASE_URL, LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN, …
```

That list is what `api` is missing in CI—not proof that `secret put` failed.

## Why `--name` didn’t apply

This line:

```bash
pnpm --filter api run deploy -- --name "$API_WORKER" --minify
```

runs the script `"deploy": "wrangler deploy --minify"`. In your log the actual command looked like:

```bash
wrangler deploy --minify -- --name api-preview-… --minify
```

The extra `--` breaks normal flag parsing, so `--name` often never reaches Wrangler. Deploy falls back to the default name `api`.

## What the fix does

```bash
pnpm --filter api exec wrangler deploy --minify --name "$API_WORKER"
```

- Calls Wrangler directly (no `package.json` `deploy` script in the middle).
- Passes `--name "$API_WORKER"` on the same command as deploy.

Now both steps target **`api-preview-dan-api-neon-setup`**:

1. `secret put … --name "$API_WORKER"` → secrets on preview worker  
2. `deploy --name "$API_WORKER"` → deploy + validate that same worker  

Wrangler’s `secrets.required` check then sees `DATABASE_URL`, `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN`, etc. on the worker you actually deploy.

## What this does *not* fix

If a GitHub secret is **empty or unset** (e.g. `LOGTAIL_SOURCE_TOKEN`), `secret put` can still fail or write an empty value—that’s separate. Your error pattern (all five required secrets “missing” at once right after `secret put`) fits the wrong-worker case, not five bad secrets.

**TL;DR:** Env vars weren’t missing from GitHub Actions; they were on preview Worker A while deploy validated default Worker `api`. One deploy command with a reliable `--name` aligns those two.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
so now it looks like the preview deploy happens whenever I push to my branch that has a PR but the cleanup only happens when the PR is closed, so wouldn't this create several deploys in neon that never get cleaned up?
</user_query>

Checking how preview naming and workflow triggers interact across pushes and PR closure.

[REDACTED]

Good instinct to check — but **each push on the same PR does not create a new Neon branch or new Workers**. It reuses the same resources and redeploys over them.

## What happens on every push

Naming is derived from **`github.head_ref`** (the PR’s branch name), not the commit or run number:

| Resource | Name | On 2nd, 3rd push… |
|----------|------|-------------------|
| Neon | `preview/<head-ref>` | Same branch (create action **reuses** it if it exists) |
| API Worker | `api-preview-<slug>` | Same script → **redeploy** |
| Web Worker | `web-preview-<slug>` | Same script → **redeploy** |

So ten pushes on `dan/api-neon-setup` → ten workflow runs, **one** Neon branch and **two** Workers, updated each time. Concurrency (`preview-${{ pr.number }}`, cancel-in-progress) only cancels overlapping runs; it doesn’t spawn extra infra.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  push1[Push 1] --> sameNeon["preview/branch"]
  push2[Push 2] --> sameNeon
  push3[Push 3] --> sameNeon
  sameNeon --> sameApi["api-preview-slug"]
  sameNeon --> sameWeb["web-preview-slug"]
  prClose[PR closed] --> cleanup[Delete branch + Workers]
```

## When things *can* leak

Cleanup only runs on `pull_request: closed`. You can still get orphans if:

1. **Cleanup fails** — delete step errors; Neon branch / Workers remain.
2. **PR closed without cleanup** — rare (disabled workflows, permissions, fork PR quirks).
3. **Branch renamed** — old name `preview/old-name` stays in Neon; new pushes use `preview/new-name`.
4. **Branch deleted on GitHub but PR wasn’t closed normally** — cleanup might not run (depends how the PR was closed).
5. **Many open PRs** — each branch gets its own `preview/*` + Workers; that’s intentional; each should be removed when **that** PR closes.

Pushes **without** an open PR do **not** run deploy-preview (trigger is only `pull_request`, not `push`).

## Summary

- **Push while PR is open:** redeploy, don’t multiply Neon branches.
- **PR closed:** cleanup should delete that branch’s Neon branch and Workers.

If you’re seeing multiple Neon branches for one PR, likely causes are renamed branches, failed cleanup runs, or old test branches from before cleanup existed — not “one branch per push.”

If you want to harden against leaks, we could add things like `expires_at` on create-branch, `delete_branch` on `pull_request` + `delete` event, or a scheduled orphan sweeper — say if you want a plan for that.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Wrangler 4.x has no `wrangler vars put` command. Setting `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` via `--var` on deploy or `wrangler secret put`.

[REDACTED]

input.old_string:
- name: Deploy web preview Worker
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
        run: pnpm --filter web exec wrangler deploy --config dist/server/wrangler.json --name "$WEB_WORKER"

      - name: Set WorkOS client ID on web preview
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
          WORKOS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID }}
        run: |
          if [[ -n "${WORKOS_CLIENT_ID:-}" ]]; then
            pnpm --filter web exec wrangler vars put WORKOS_CLIENT_ID "$WORKOS_CLIENT_ID" --name "$WEB_WORKER"
          fi
input.new_string:
- name: Deploy web preview Worker
        env:
          WEB_WORKER: ${{ steps.names.outputs.web_worker }}
          WORKOS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.WORKOS_CLIENT_ID }}
        run: |
          deploy_args=(--config dist/server/wrangler.json --name "$WEB_WORKER")
          if [[ -n "${WORKOS_CLIENT_ID:-}" ]]; then
            deploy_args+=(--var "WORKOS_CLIENT_ID:${WORKOS_CLIENT_ID}")
          fi
          pnpm --filter web exec wrangler deploy "${deploy_args[@]}"

input.old_string:
```bash
# Example: staging
pnpm --filter web exec wrangler vars put WORKOS_CLIENT_ID "client_..." --env staging
```
input.new_string:
```bash
# Example: staging (Wrangler 4: use --var on deploy, or secret put)
pnpm --filter web exec wrangler deploy --env staging --var "WORKOS_CLIENT_ID:client_..."
# Or after deploy: echo "client_..." | pnpm --filter web exec wrangler secret put WORKOS_CLIENT_ID --env staging
```

content.text:
The problem was **`wrangler vars put` doesn’t exist in Wrangler 4** (your catalog version). Wrangler treated `vars`, `put`, and the client id as unknown positional args, which produced that error.

**Change:** set `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` on deploy with `--var` (supported on `wrangler deploy`):

```bash
wrangler deploy --config dist/server/wrangler.json --name "$WEB_WORKER" --var "WORKOS_CLIENT_ID:..."
```

The separate “Set WorkOS” step was removed; the web deploy step applies the var when the GitHub secret is set. [`apps/web/README.md`](apps/web/README.md) was updated so staging examples don’t reference `vars put`.

Re-run the preview workflow; the web step should pass (or skip WorkOS if `WORKOS_CLIENT_ID` isn’t configured).
